outputs.loading_convention: Vanilla <script> tag exposes window.RulesEngine; also CommonJS-friendly via module.exports when present (used by edge-case-testing's node sandbox).

error_contract.controller_guidance: Controller should wrap applyMove in try/catch on user clicks (defense in depth) or pre-check via legalMoves. The renderer's filled/disabled gating already prevents most illegal-click paths.

contract_compliance.rules-engine--ai-opponent.json: matches: createBoard/applyMove/checkOutcome/legalMoves shapes verified against contract spec
contract_compliance.rules-engine--controller-and-shell.json: matches: same module surface used by both consumers

notes: Pure functions: applyMove uses cloneBoard so input state is not mutated.
WINNING_LINES covers all 8 lines (3 rows, 3 cols, 2 diagonals).
legalMoves returns ascending order, satisfying the deterministic-order requirement.
Indexing is row-major: 0,1,2 top row; 3,4,5 middle; 6,7,8 bottom.
